openmeter/debug/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP handler layer for the debug domain, bridging the debug.DebugConnector service to HTTP using the httptransport pattern. Single endpoint: GetMetrics, which resolves namespace from context and returns plain-text ClickHouse metrics.

patterns.name: Handler interface + struct separation
patterns.description: Expose a DebugHandler interface with method GetMetrics(); back it with a private debugHandler struct. Consumers depend on the interface, not the concrete type.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for every endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint is returned as an httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params] value — decode, operation, encode wired inline. Never implement http.Handler directly.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder
patterns.description: Namespace is never read from URL params or headers directly; always use h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx). Missing namespace → 500 internal error.

patterns.name: Per-handler error encoder appended via httptransport.AppendOptions
patterns.description: Domain-specific error mapping (e.g. validation → 400) is added per-handler by appending httptransport.WithErrorEncoder to the shared options slice, not by replacing them.

patterns.name: Constructor accepts variadic HandlerOptions
patterns.description: NewDebugHandler takes ...httptransport.HandlerOption and stores them on the struct so all handlers inherit shared options (auth, tracing, etc.) from the router layer.
patterns.example: func NewDebugHandler(dec namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder, conn debug.DebugConnector, options ...httptransport.HandlerOption) DebugHandler

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; defines the DebugHandler interface, private struct, constructor, request/response types, and the GetMetrics handler factory.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetMetricsHandlerResponse is a type alias for string — uses PlainTextResponseEncoder, not JSON. Adding a new endpoint must follow the same HandlerWithArgs shape; do not return raw http.HandlerFunc.

anti_patterns: Implementing http.Handler directly instead of using httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs
Reading namespace from URL path or query params instead of namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder
Replacing h.options instead of appending with httptransport.AppendOptions when adding error encoders
Putting business logic (metrics computation) inside the decode or encode functions — delegate to debugConnector
Returning JSON from a plain-text endpoint by swapping the encoder without updating the Content-Type error path

decisions.decision: Handler returns httptransport.HandlerWithArgs value, not http.Handler
decisions.rationale: httptransport provides uniform decode/encode/error pipeline with OTel and error-encoder chaining across all domain httpdriver packages.

decisions.decision: Namespace resolved from context via NamespaceDecoder, not from request
decisions.rationale: Static namespace injection (self-hosted) is handled at the router middleware layer; the handler must not re-parse it, ensuring multi-tenant isolation is enforced once and consistently.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a second debug endpoint (e.g. GetStatus)
